hook: You already know how to solve equations with one unknown — but what happens when two quantities are changing together, like the cost of two different phone plans or the speed of two runners? A single equation can't capture the full picture on its own. In this unit, you'll discover how pairing two equations unlocks answers that neither equation could reveal alone.
concept: The unifying idea is that many real situations involve two unknowns that must satisfy two conditions at the same time — and finding where both conditions are met simultaneously is called solving a 'system of linear equations.'

topic_roadmap.topic_name: Introduction to Systems and Graphing Solutions
topic_roadmap.description: You will learn to recognize a system of two linear equations and identify its solution as the point where both lines intersect on a graph.
Master solving a system algebraically by isolating one variable and substituting its expression into the other equation to find an exact solution.
You will learn to add or subtract strategically scaled equations to eliminate one variable and solve for the other efficiently.
Discover how to recognize and interpret systems whose lines are parallel (no solution) or identical (infinitely many solutions) from both algebraic and graphical perspectives.
Apply substitution and elimination to solve real-world problems involving mixtures, rates, and costs by setting up and solving systems from written descriptions.
You will learn to graph two linear inequalities on the same coordinate plane and identify the overlapping shaded region that represents all solutions satisfying both conditions.
